creator: peter@FreeBSD.org
text:
"dedup: DDT entries 1415219, size 2676 on disk, 540 in core"

->  728MB of ram to cache this.  Fortunately that's a relatively small portion of your system ram.

I would still be doing a zfs set dedup=off anywhere it's on.  While it won't fix the on-disk dedup corruption, at least you won't risk adding to it any more.

Your system is crashing trying to parse the on-disk dedup records.  That's what the *_ddt_* functions are about in the traces.  I can't imagine this ending well.

Does a scrub find the problem? or does the scrub trigger the assertions?
